video:video.video:title: A rare double tornado seen in Sweden
video:video.video:description: A rare double tornado was caught on camera swirling over water in Sweden on Saturday. The two funnels later merged into one as the storm passed over Grästorp.

video:video.video:title: CENTCOM video released as US launches more airstrikes against Iran
video:video.video:description: The latest attacks again showed how, step by step, the U.S. and Iran have inched closer to all-out war as last month’s interim deal meant to permanently end the fighting has crumbled and shipping traffic in the Strait of Hormuz has largely stalled.

video:video.video:title: Trump celebrates World Cup success, comments on action against Iran
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump hailed the "incredible" success of the 2026 World Cup after attending the final of the tournament Sunday.

video:video.video:title: Why Britain is getting a new prime minister without a general election
video:video.video:description: Veteran Labour Party politician and popular former Greater Manchester mayor Andy Burnham became Britain’s next prime minister on Monday, formally taking over from Keir Starmer, who announced his resignation last month.

video:video.video:title: Shakira, Madonna take the World Cup stage, along with BTS and Bieber
video:video.video:description: World Cup Final debuts Super Bowl-style halftime show with Madonna, BTS, Bieber and Shakira. (July 20)

video:video.video:title: Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan arrested in Miami, US Marshals service tells AP
video:video.video:description: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate have been arrested by federal authorities in Miami. A spokesperson for the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Argentina and Spain fans descend on New York for World Cup final
video:video.video:description: Jubilant Spain and Argentina fans were full of joy Saturday, dancing and singing in New York ahead of the World Cup final on Sunday.

video:video.video:title: South Korea awaits UNESCO decision on biodiversity haven Garorim Bay
video:video.video:description: South Korea's tidal flats are renowned for their wealth of biodiversity, some are inscribed on UNESCO's World Heritage List, but one has been overlooked until now.

video:video.video:title: 1 killed, 8 injured in an overnight Russian attack on Kyiv
video:video.video:description: One person was killed and eight others injured in an overnight Russian attack on Kyiv that included ballistic missiles, local authorities said.

video:video.video:title: The AI voice app that detects COPD flare-ups earlier
video:video.video:description: AI could help COPD patients check for flare-ups from home. It analyzes a patient's voice to determine if the incurable lung condition is about to become worse.

video:video.video:title: Italy's historic 'people's theatres' hope for UNESCO World Heritage status
video:video.video:description: Italy's stunning community theatres are hoping for UNESCO World Heritage recognition this weekend. The cultural centres were built during the 18th and 19th centuries and still entertain local people to this day.

video:video.video:title: Rescuers search rubble after 5.5 magnitude earthquake hits Peru's Andes region, killing at least 5
video:video.video:description: Rescuers search rubble after 5.5 magnitude earthquake hits Peru's Andes region, killing at least 5

video:video.video:title: Video circulating appears to show moment Iran missiles hit Jordan base, killing 2 US service members
video:video.video:description: Video footage circulating online appears to show the moment Iranian missiles hit an airbase in Jordan, killing two American service members.

video:video.video:title: Video circulating appears to show moment Iran missiles hit Jordan base, killing 2 US service members
video:video.video:description: Video footage circulating online appears to show the moment Iranian missiles hit an airbase in Jordan, killing two American service members.

video:video.video:title: Soccer fans flock to 2026 World Cup final
video:video.video:description: Thousands of fans lined up hours before the 2026 World Cup final hoping to watch their team hoist the FIFA World Cup Trophy. (AP Video: Emily Wang)

video:video.video:title: Trump flies over stadium in Marine One before attending the World Cup final
video:video.video:description: The U.S. presidential helicopter, Marine One, flew over the stadium hosting the World Cup final, heralding the arrival of President Donald Trump. (AP video: Emily Wang)

video:video.video:title: JD and Usha Vance welcome a baby boy, the first child born to a sitting VP in more than 150 years
video:video.video:description: Vice President JD Vance and second lady Usha Vance have welcomed their fourth child. Their son Alec Neel Vance is the first child born to a sitting vice president in more than 150 years.

video:video.video:title: Sailboat with humanitarian aid arrives in Havana as Cuba's crisis deepens
video:video.video:description: Sailboat with humanitarian aid arrives in Havana as Cuba's crisis deepens

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories July 20
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Monday July 20th: Ninth straight night of U.S. strikes on Iran; Third U.S. service member killed in Middle East; Andrew and Tristan Tate due in court in Miami; Spain wins World Cup.

video:video.video:title: Wild celebrations in Madrid after Spain seal World Cup victory
video:video.video:description: Wild celebrations in Madrid after Spain seal World Cup victory

video:video.video:title: Israeli strike destroys residential building in Deir al-Balah, central Gaza Strip
video:video.video:description: Israel’s military destroyed a residential building in Deir Al-Balah, central of Gaza Strip after ordering people to flee their houses, residents said.

video:video.video:title: Cockroach movement gathers in New Delhi to demand resignation of education minister
video:video.video:description: Supporters of India’s youth-led Cockroach movement clashed with police Monday as thousands of them tried to march to Parliament, demanding the education minister’s resignation.

video:video.video:title: Indian police clash with Cockroach Party supporters near Parliament
video:video.video:description: Thousands of supporters of the youth-led Cockroach movement gathered Monday to march to India's Parliament, demanding the resignation of the education minister despite police denying permission for the protest, as the government appeared to open its first talks with movement leaders.

video:video.video:title: Cuban artist and dissident exiled from country after 5 years in prison arrives in US
video:video.video:description: Famous Cuban dissident artist, Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara, arrived in Miami on Saturday after being released from a five-year prison sentence on the condition that he leave his country.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 19
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Sunday, July 19: US launches more airstrikes at Iran; a 5.5 magnitude quake shakes Peru; Recue crews work to find landslide survivors in China; FIFA World Cup final kicks off in New Jersey

video:video.video:title: What fans will remember most about the World Cup
video:video.video:description: From unforgettable matches to breakout stars and shared watch parties, fans reflected on the moments that made this World Cup one to remember.

video:video.video:title: Rescuers find 5 survivors and are still looking for 20 others after boat sinks in Indonesia
video:video.video:description: Rescuers on Saturday evening found five survivors, including a 7-year-old girl, from a passenger boat that sank earlier in the week in eastern Indonesia waters and were searching for 20 other people still missing.

video:video.video:title: Why Britain is getting a new prime minister without a general election
video:video.video:description: Veteran Labour Party politician and popular former Greater Manchester mayor Andy Burnham is set to become Britain’s next prime minister on Monday, formally taking over from Keir Starmer, who announced his resignation last month.

video:video.video:title: Fans react outside the stadium after Spain win the World Cup by beating Argentina 1-0
video:video.video:description: Soccer fans reacted on Sunday after watching Spain beat Argentina to win the World Cup for a second time in its history. (AP video shot by Emily Wang)

video:video.video:title: Larry the cat welcomes another PM to Downing Street
video:video.video:description: As British Prime Minister Keir Starmer steps down after just two years in office, he will hand over power to a new Labour Party leader, Andy Burnham.

video:video.video:title: Spain's World Cup champions return home to Madrid
video:video.video:description: Spain's World Cup winning team returned home on Monday afternoon. The squad will be greeted by the Spanish royal palace and Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez before boarding an open-top bus for a parade through central Madrid in the evening.

video:video.video:title: LA medical examiner wants death details in D4vd case and others to be public
video:video.video:description: Los Angeles County's chief medical examiner Dr. Odey Ukpo is emphasizing the need for greater transparency when it comes to autopsies following celebrity deaths.

video:video.video:title: Rescuers search rubble after an earthquake hits Peru’s Andes region and kills at least 6
video:video.video:description: A 5.5 magnitude earthquake shook the Andes region of Peru, killing at least six people, injuring more than 30 and displacing 300, authorities said Sunday.

video:video.video:title: Starmer delivers final speech before leaving for Buckingham Palace to tender formal resignation
video:video.video:description: Outgoing British Prime Minister Keir Starmer bade farewell outside 10 Downing Street on Monday. Starmer was forced to resign by his own party after missteps that eroded his standing with lawmakers and the public.

video:video.video:title: Andy Burnham arrives at Downing St after being appointed as UK PM
video:video.video:description: Andy Burnham, the former mayor of Greater Manchester and new leader of the Labour Party, took the final steps on Monday to succeed Keir Starmer and become Britain's seventh prime minister in a decade.

video:video.video:title: At least 3 killed, 45 injured in Russian airstrikes in Zaporizhzhia, Ukraine officials say
video:video.video:description: At least 3 killed, 45 injured in Russian airstrikes in Zaporizhzhia, Ukraine officials say

video:video.video:title: Andy Burnham gives first speech as UK prime minister at Downing Street
video:video.video:description: Britain’s political turbulence has given way, at least temporarily, to smooth choreography as Andy Burnham succeeded Keir Starmer, becoming the seventh U.K. prime minister since 2016.

video:video.video:title: Nolan’s 'The Odyssey' storms the box office with a $264.1 million global debut
video:video.video:description: Christopher Nolan’s “The Odyssey” has stormed the box office with global earnings of $264.1 million - even better than the filmmaker's “Oppenheimer” debut. (July 20)

video:video.video:title: Land artist in Italy celebrates Spain's 2026 World Cup win
video:video.video:description: Artist Dario Gambarin has created an image of the World Cup 2026 trophy by using a tractor to carve the artwork out of his field near Verona in northern Italy.

video:video.video:title: Israeli strike destroys residential building in central Gaza Strip
video:video.video:description: Israel’s military destroyed a residential building in Deir Al-Balah, central of Gaza Strip after ordering people to flee their houses, residents said.

video:video.video:title: Londoners give thoughts on Andy Burnham becoming UK prime minister
video:video.video:description: Commuters in central London gave a mixed but broadly open-minded response on Monday to Andy Burnham becoming prime minister, with several saying they would give the new Labour leader a chance while acknowledging they knew little of his platform.

video:video.video:title: Excavation team operates in southern Taiwan to recover Japanese WWII soldiers' remains
video:video.video:description: Heads down in a bow, a handful of experts offered their respects on Monday ahead of an excavation aimed at recovering WWII Japanese soldier remains in southern Taiwan.

video:video.video:title: Incendiary device erupts outside New York federal building; FBI says a person is in custody
video:video.video:description: The building at 26 Federal Plaza houses a number of government offices — including the New York office of the FBI — and an immigration court where many migrants have been arrested after leaving hearings.

video:video.video:title: 300 humanoid robots show they can do more than dance
video:video.video:description: Dancing robots are always a crowd pleaser, but at the WAIC tech event Saturday the focus was on showing that the machines are not for entertainment, but executing practical tasks.

video:video.video:title: Britain's new leader Andy Burnham outlines ambitious plans after taking office
video:video.video:description: Britain's new Prime Minister Andy Burnham has formally taken office, promising to tackle the cost-of-living crisis, revive British industry and return more powers to local government.

video:video.video:title: Person in custody after incendiary device was deployed outside New York federal building
video:video.video:description: A person who authorities say deployed an incendiary device outside a New York federal building Monday, the FBI said.

video:video.video:title: Rev. Al Sharpton offers $50k for information on Mississippi teen who was found dead after boat trip
video:video.video:description: The Rev. Al Sharpton officiated the funeral for Nolan Wells, the 18-year-old, whose death following a July 4 boat trip with friends has captured national attention and prompted calls for an independent investigation.

video:video.video:title: In one of Kyiv's most frequently bombed neighborhoods, residents choose to stay
video:video.video:description: Residents of Kyiv’s historic Lukianivka neighborhood share a dark joke: Russian strikes hit the area so frequently, they ought to be issued military service documents as though they are living on the front line of the war.

video:video.video:title: Sirens heard in Amman, as Jordan says Iran launches another missile attack
video:video.video:description: Jordan’s armed forces said Monday evening they had shot down three Iranian missiles, adding that there had been no material damage or casualties.

video:video.video:title: Lawyer for Andrew and Tristan Tate says they will fight extradition to the UK
video:video.video:description: The Tates appeared in federal court Monday for the first time since their arrest on Saturday and are being held in federal detention.

video:video.video:title: US gas prices back up to an average of $4 a gallon again as the US and Iran launch attacks
video:video.video:description: Patrick De Haan, head of petroleum analysis at GasBuddy, talks about gas prices climbing back up to an average of $4 a gallon as the US and Iran war continues.

video:video.video:title: Suspect in custody after starting fire outside New York federal building, FBI says
video:video.video:description: 43-year-old Andrew Arrabaca, who was a mechanic on Patriot missile systems, is accused of setting off fireworks and lighting a fire Monday morning in front of the Javits Federal Building in New York.

video:video.video:title: Women in Bangolo, South Sudan discuss hardship and uncertain future, amid conflict in area
video:video.video:description: Women in the remote village of Bangolo in South Sudan's Western Equatoria State tell United Nations police officers that the lack of healthcare and education continue to threaten their lives and livelihoods.

video:video.video:title: Aftermath of fatal Israel strikes in Gaza City and south of Strip
video:video.video:description: Israeli strikes in Gaza City and southern Gaza Monday killed at least four Palestinians and wounded over a dozen others, according to local health officials.

video:video.video:title: Nolan Wells' parents speak at teen's funeral
video:video.video:description: Family and supporters of Nolan Xavier Wells gathered in a Mississippi church Monday to mourn and celebrate the 18-year-old, whose death following a July 4 boat trip with friends has captured national attention.

video:video.video:title: Argentina's Paredes pushes Gavi to ground, overshadowing Spain's full-time celebrations
video:video.video:description: Argentina's Leandro Paredes received a red card after full-time in Sunday's World Cup final for pushing over Gavi as Spain’s 1-0 victory in New Jersey was followed by an on-pitch melee.

video:video.video:title: Spain team arrives home after winning the World Cup
video:video.video:description: The 2026 World Cup champions returned to Spain on Monday. The squad will be greeted by King Felipe VI and Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez before boarding an open-top bus for a parade through central Madrid in the evening.

video:video.video:title: Attempt to save a struggling swimmer leaves 5 dead in an Ohio river
video:video.video:description: Authorities say five people died on a river in Ohio after several of them went into the water to assist another person who was struggling to swim.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 20
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Monday, July 20th: U.S. and Iran escalate hostilities in Middle East; Man taken into custody after deploying incendiary device outside federal building in NYC; Great Britain has a new prime minister; Spain's national soccer team returns home as heroes.

video:video.video:title: Spain fans in Madrid celebrate World Cup win
video:video.video:description: Thousands of Spain supporters crowded the streets of Madrid in the early hours of Monday, celebrating the country's victory in the World Cup final on Sunday.

video:video.video:title: Guyana busca sobrevivientes tras naufragio de ferry; investigan las causas
video:video.video:description: Equipos de rescate en Guyana buscaron sobrevivientes el lunes y las autoridades iniciaron una investigación después que un transbordador, que se cree iba sobrecargado, volcara el fin de semana.

video:video.video:title: Spain's World Cup winning team throw Luis de la Fuente in the air during celebration in Madrid
video:video.video:description: As part of the festivities the players and their coaching team took to a giant stage in Cebeles Square, lifting the trophy and dancing and singing as they were greeted by loud cheers.

video:video.video:title: Kevin Hart says attending the World Cup was 'amazing'
video:video.video:description: Kevin Hart was joined by a slew of celebrities in New Jersey-- right outside New York-- for the final World Cup game Sunday, as Spain beat Argentina 1-0. (July 20)

video:video.video:title: Marcello Hernández on working with fellow 'SNL' actors on new film
video:video.video:description: Marcello Hernández jokes fellow "Saturday Night Live" castmate Ben Marshall was 'in danger' in the Miami sun while filming their new Netflix comedy, "72 Hours."

video:video.video:title: US conducts latest round of airstrikes against Iran while another tanker attacked in Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: The United States conducted yet-another round of airstrikes targeting Iran on Tuesday. The strikes came as Iran attacked a tanker in the Strait of Hormuz early Tuesday, forcing its crew to abandon the ship.

video:video.video:title: Guyana launches criminal probe as crews scour waters for survivors after ferry capsizes
video:video.video:description: Rescuers in Guyana searched for survivors Monday as authorities launched an investigation after a ferry believed to have been overloaded capsized over the weekend.

video:video.video:title: Ski enthusiasts flock to sub-Saharan Africa and a snowy mountain resort in Lesotho
video:video.video:description: Lesotho's high elevation and mountainous terrain combine to make it the coldest country on the continent, with temperatures in the highlands typically plunging to minus 8 degrees Celsius (about 18 degrees Fahrenheit) between June and August.
